{{Quote|I feel we must abide by the Council's decision. That is the way of the Order.|Qual|<ref name="shadows" />}}
'''Qual''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served on the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|Jedi Council]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine's]] [[Jedi Enclave]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Qual was active on the Enclave Council during the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]] of [[3993 BBY]], when [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] slaying [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-created beasts left over from the war. After the official end of the Great Hunt that year, Qual was present when the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Enclave Council notified [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Duron Qel-Droma]], [[Shaela Nuur]], and [[Guun Han Saresh]] that they were being sent to [[Cleansing of Kashyyyk|cleanse]] the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] of [[Terentatek/Legends|terentateks]]. Although some on the Council disagreed with the mission, Qual saw it to be tactically sound and believed in the wisdom of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], which had chosen the three Jedi. However, Qel-Droma, Nuur, and Saresh never returned.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I have some reservations as well. However, as Qual said, we must abide by the council's decisions—and have ''faith'' in their wisdom.|Aleco Stusea|<ref name="shadows" />}}
[[File:Qual.jpg|thumb|left|Qual on the Jedi Enclave Council]]
A [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Qual was, by [[3993 BBY]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and a member of the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|Jedi Council]] of the [[Jedi Enclave]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. In this position, Qual served alongside respected [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Masters [[Vrook Lamar]], [[Aleco Stusea]], and [[Bala Nisi]]. In 3993 BBY, an official end was called to the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]]—after the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] that ended in [[3996 BBY]], many [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] had scoured worlds such as [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], and [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] for [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-created monstrosities that still posed threats to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the general populace. Two [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the supposed end of the Great Hunt, Qual and the Jedi Enclave Council called three such Knights—[[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Duron Qel-Droma]] and [[Shaela Nuur]], and [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Guun Han Saresh]], considered to be the Order's finest hunters. The three Knights were told that they had been handpicked by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to go to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], a planet deeply steeped in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], and [[Cleansing of Kashyyyk|cleanse]] the world of the vicious [[Terentatek/Legends|terentatek]] beasts.<ref name="shadows" />

After the hunters departed, Qual discussed the mission with his fellow Council members. Although Nisi and Lamar disagreed with sending only three Jedi to a world with such a strong dark side taint as Korriban, Qual felt that they needed to abide by the Jedi High Council's wisdom, since a large party of Jedi on Korriban was too much of a risk. Qual's assertions were backed by Stusea, although she admitted her own reservations about the mission. However, Qual's relative optimism did not prove warranted, as none of the three Jedi sent to Korriban ever returned.<ref name="shadows" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Coruscant does not want a large party of Jedi on Korriban—too much risk.|Qual|<ref name="shadows" />}}
A Selkath with [[Color/Legends|blue]] skin and blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], Qual trusted in the wisdom of the Jedi High Council. As such, he held a more positive opinion of the mission to Korriban, as he believed that the High Council's decision was the tactically sound way to deal with the problem. Furthermore, Qual believed that following the word of the Jedi High Council was the way of the Jedi Order itself.<ref name="shadows" />

==Behind the scenes==
Qual first appeared in "[[Shadows and Light]]," one of the stories featured in [[Star Wars Tales 23|''Star Wars Tales'' 23]], written by [[Joshua Ortega]] and released in [[2005]]. In the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]], the character was illustrated by [[Dustin Weaver]].<ref name="shadows" />
